Patch Set 7:

please explain why it's needed in the first place.

for mkdir it's enough to just ignore -EEXIST (fid is unique) ?

for rmdir it's enough to ignore -ENOENT and destroy the target object?

probably just need to ensure remote part is committed ?

Patch Set 7:

This is what exactly this patch does. Checking object exists for create resend RPC, and checking index exist for resend unlink RPC. 

Yes, the remote part is all synced. Or I miss your point here?

Patch Set 7:

isn't this enough to see -EEXIST in osp_trans_start() ? why do you need to pass the flags through the stack and in the RPC ?

Patch Set 7:

I need make sure this is checking during resend rpc, instead of any other RPC.

Patch Set 7: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(9 inline comments)

Seems like there are several cleanups needed here that could be done before Phase II, even if they are not strictly necessary today.

Could you please file a bug, and hopefully they can be fixed before final release.
Patch Set 7: (2 inline comments)

Patch Set 7:

I hope that we can separate recovery protocol from low-level protocol (basically transferring updates in terms of OSD API). the intention here is to be able to develop them separately and keep low-level protocol stable/simple for long.

in turn, the recovery protocol will evolve:
in Phase 1 this protocol is basically no-op,
in Phase 2 it's supposed to implement two-phase-commit approach
in ... it will likely be epoch/undo based

but the updates are supposed to be the same, I think, except rather minor extentions/improvements.

Patch Set 9:

a possible solution to that destroy-with-create problem is to introduce something like a transaction # into update rpc. so that we can put few atomic sets (transactions) into a single RPC. then out_handle() would do essentially the same as now, but for every few updates marked with the same transaction #: grab objects, declare, execute, release objects.

Patch Set 14: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

I still don't really see the benefit of destroying the old object and immediately recreating it in osp_md_declare_object_create(). When we get async updates in DNE phase II this will be the wrong thing to do, since the object may have has other updates (e.g. files created in the remote directory). If the object exists, it means that the remote updates have all been committed (since they are sent in a single batch) so nothing else should be needed?

Patch Set 14:

the benefit is that from the layers above (mdt/mdd) nothing changes whether object has been already created or not. IOW,  the high level logic is still the same and  "recovery" is hidden within OSP. as part of that recovery we send RPC  again and get the reply when the object creation has been committed.

Patch Set 14: Verified

parents.subject: LU-2148 kernel: Kernel update for latest FC18 kernel

commit.subject: LU-1187 out: add resend check for update.
commit.message:
LU-1187 out: add resend check for update.

1. Add update resend check between MDTs.

2. Even during creating the new object, osp_init_object
   still needs to get remote object attrs, because the
   object might be created already by some partial failed
   operation.

3. During resend handling, OSP needs to delete the existing
   orphan objects first, then do remote create.

4. MDT will check whether the name has been deleted during
   the resend of remote unlink, and only delete the local
   directory if the name on the remote MDT has been delete.

5. Fix the fail_id assignment location to only fail real
   update RPC.

6. Some fixes for replay DNE test sctipts.

Change-Id: Ia9ba8091b6622b0e2fd1f1b4fd355b5ff3eb9758
Signed-off-by: Wang Di <di.wang@intel.com>
